currency.source: Bank of Zambia (boz.zm), Wise, exchange-rates.org, valutafx.com
currency.baseNote: Rates show ZMW per 1 unit of foreign currency. ↑ ZMW = Kwacha weakening (depreciating).
currency.mpRateNote: Monetary Policy Rate reduced 75bps from 14.25% on Feb 11, 2026. BOZ held at 13.5% at May review. Next review: August 2026.

USD.note: BOZ official mid-rate (Jun 9): 17.7050. Wise mid-market Jun 11: 17.46 (1 ZMW = 0.05728 USD). Kwacha strengthened ~0.71% from Jun 10. 7-day high 0.0573, low 0.0555 USD/ZMW. YTD appreciation ~21%.

GBP.note: 1 GBP = 23.27 ZMW (Wise Jun 11). 1 ZMW = 0.04297 GBP. GBP/ZMW down 2.3% day-on-day reflecting broad kwacha strength. 7-day range: 23.27–24.91.

EUR.note: 1 EUR = 20.09 ZMW (Wise Jun 11). 1 ZMW = 0.04977 EUR. EUR/ZMW -2.3% day-on-day. 7-day high 20.82, low 20.09. YTD decline ~21%.

ZAR.note: 1 ZAR = 1.057 ZMW (Wise Jun 11). 1 ZMW = 0.9463 ZAR. Kwacha gained ~2.0% vs rand day-on-day. 7-day ZMW/ZAR high 0.9466, low 0.9200.

CNY.note: Chinese yuan. Zambia's growing yuan-denominated mining tax corridor.

trendAnalysis.direction: Kwacha strengthens — USD/ZMW mid 17.46 (Wise), BOZ official 17.7050
drivers: Record copper output 890K tonnes (2025) — strong export earnings
Record maize harvest >4M tonnes — reduced food import needs
Debt restructuring 94% complete — sovereign risk premium collapsing
IMF successor programme talks scheduled July 2026 — staff visit confirmed
MPR at 13.5% — high real yields attracting carry trade inflows
Yuan mining tax corridor reducing USD demand
Gross international reserves at historic high of US$6.5 billion
trendAnalysis.outlook: BOZ held MPR at 13.5% at May review. Inflation at 6.8% (April). Kwacha continues to strengthen backed by strong copper exports, high reserves, and capital inflows. Further appreciation likely if copper holds above $9,500/tonne and IMF successor programme progresses. BOZ may lean against excessive strength to protect export competitiveness.
trendAnalysis.projection3m: USD/ZMW 16.50–18.00 range. Bias toward further appreciation if copper holds above $9,500/tonne and IMF successor programme progresses.
trendAnalysis.projection6m: USD/ZMW 16.00–19.00 range. Wider range reflects election uncertainty (Aug 2026). Post-election policy continuity would support kwacha; fiscal slippage would reverse gains.
